text:
🇹🇭 THAILAND — TRANSACTION COSTS 
1. Why this matters
Defines total acquisition and transfer cost, distinguishing mandatory government charges from market and optional costs.
2. Official Government Fees (Mandatory)
Transfer Fee
(Official Fact)
Typically 2% of the appraised property value
Payable at the Land Department upon transfer
Stamp Duty
(Official Fact)
0.5% of transaction value
Applicable when Specific Business Tax (SBT) is not applied
Withholding Tax
(Official Fact)
Withheld at the time of transfer
Calculated based on appraised value or sale price
Treated as advance payment of income tax
Specific Business Tax (SBT)
(Official Fact)
Approximately 3.3% (including local tax)
Applies if property is held for less than 5 years
3. Common Market Practice Costs (Negotiable)
(Market Practice)
Real Estate Agent Fees:
Typically ~3% of property value
Usually paid by seller (may vary by agreement)
4. Optional Professional Costs
(Market Practice / Optional)
Legal / Due Diligence Fees
Vary depending on transaction complexity
Commonly used for title verification and contract drafting
(Advisory Note)
Not legally mandatory
Strongly recommended in practice
5. Advisory Caution
(Advisory Caution)
Transaction costs vary depending on:
Holding period (affects SBT applicability)
Property value (affects fee calculations)
Allocation of costs between buyer and seller
(Advisory Caution)
Multiple taxes and fees apply simultaneously, making total cost transaction-specific.
6. What Needs Confirmation (Transaction-Level)
Applicability of SBT vs stamp duty
Withholding tax calculation method
Transfer fee allocation between parties
Legal and agent fee agreements
7. Sources
https://www.dol.go.th
https://www.rd.go.th
8. Verification Date
07/04/2026
9. Escalation Note
ESCALATION REQUIRED –
Transaction costs depend on holding period, tax applicability (SBT vs stamp duty), and allocation between parties, requiring transaction-specific confirmation.
